mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on future equity cash flows and signals easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the cut is part of an easing cycle rather than a one-off move.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are negative or below unconditional median after FEDFUNDS declines for three consecutive monthly observations, or if triggered periods do not outperform non-triggered periods over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a prior-month threshold signals labor-market deterioration, which weakens household income expectations and raises recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of episodes where UNRATE has risen more than 0.3pp over 3 months, or if the signal's Rank IC is not negative in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both accelerate together, rising payroll income supports retail sales and near-term revenue expectations, creating a positive demand-led impulse for S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint high-growth observations in RSAFS and PAYEMS do not produce higher 20-day forward returns than either series alone, or if the combined signal loses positive Rank IC for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained monthly decline in INDPRO indicates industrial production weakness and tighter effective supply conditions, which depresses cyclical earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with falling INDPRO over two consecutive months are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns more than half the time, or if the signal's rolling 3-year correlation with forward returns is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts collapse below a recent trend threshold marks a demand shock that spills into construction, credit, and consumer confidence, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average by more than 10%
failure_condition: Reject if sub-trend HOUST observations are not associated with below-median 20-day returns, or if the trigger fails to improve directional accuracy versus a no-trigger baseline over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of falling CPIAUCSL and rising FEDFUNDS captures a real-policy tightening shock: inflation cools but policy remains restrictive, compressing multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint sign pattern of CPIAUCSL down and FEDFUNDS up does not precede negative 5-day returns more often than chance, or if the interaction term is not stronger than either proxy alone in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary labor market, payroll losses in PAYEMS combined with a rising UNRATE indicate falling aggregate income and stronger downside equity pressure, so the S&P 500 should underperform over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous PAYEMS deterioration and UNRATE increases fail to predict negative 60-day returns, or if the interaction produces no improvement over single-variable labor signals in any 2-year rolling test.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive and RSAFS growth is simultaneously strong, the economy is expanding on both output and spending dimensions, which supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive co-movement in GDPC1 and RSAFS does not correspond to above-median 60-day returns, or if the joint signal ceases to outperform unconditional returns over any 4-year rolling window.
